message:
fix(remote+error): Saga Drop panicking guard + circuit breaker Acquire ordering

Two cross-cutting safety issues found via systematic codebase analysis:

1. HIGH — remote.rs Saga Drop: executed user-provided compensation
   closures without checking std::thread::panicking(). If a closure
   panicked during an existing unwind, the process would abort.
   Fix: check panicking() and skip compensations during unwind,
   matching the pattern used by all other intentional-panic Drop
   impls in the codebase.

2. MEDIUM — error/recovery.rs circuit breaker: state() loaded with
   Ordering::Relaxed but transition() wrote with Ordering::Release.
   On weakly-ordered architectures (ARM), a thread could miss a
   state transition and fail to count successes/failures correctly.
   Fix: state() now uses Acquire, transition() uses AcqRel/Acquire,
   ensuring proper happens-before between state transitions and
   subsequent reads.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
